tripura-sundari-pancharatnam: Five jewelled verses praising Tripura Sundari, ruler of the three cities
Draws the grace of Lalita as the supreme Shri Vidya goddess
Surrounds the devotee with the auspicious protection of the Mother
Recited to clear inner faults and deepen Shri Vidya practice
Suited to Friday worship and Navratri offerings to the Devi
tripura-sundari-sahasranama: Thousand names of Tripura Sundari from Brahmanda Purana
Supreme text of Shri Vidya tradition for divine grace
Invokes the blessing of beauty, prosperity, and all auspiciousness
Invokes liberation and ultimate spiritual realization
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of material and spiritual desires
Chanted through Navratri and on Lalita Panchami for the Devi
tripura-sundari-stotram: Hymn to Tripura Sundari, the Supreme Goddess of Shri Vidya
Invokes the blessing of beauty, grace, and charm to the devotee
Calls down saubhagya and marital fortune from the Mother's grace
Invokes the combined power of Lakshmi, Kali, and Saraswati
Sacred for Shri Vidya upasana and Lalita worship
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires through the grace of rajarajeshvari
vaidyanath-stotram: Invokes healing from all diseases and ailments
Equivalent to pilgrimage to Vaidyanath, the ninth Jyotirlinga
Chanted to dissolve past misdeeds before the divine physician Shiva
Opens the path to Shivaloka for the departing soul
Grants both worldly wishes and final liberation through Vaidyanath Shiva
vamana-stotram: Invokes the expansive power of Lord Trivikrama
Praises Vamana's dwarf form to humble pride and ego in the heart
Invokes the merit of charitable giving (dana)
Honours Vamana, who measured the worlds, to wash away wrongdoing
Recited toward Vishnuloka and final release for the devotee
Traditionally invoked for protection from enemies and grants victory
vasudeva-stotram: Reveals the supreme nature of Vasudeva as the cause of all causes
Contains key Gita teachings on surrender to the divine
Recited to purify the heart by surrender at Vasudeva's feet
Invokes deep devotion and understanding of vasudeva tattva
Leads to Vaikuntha (the supreme abode of Vishnu)
Ideal for daily recitation and philosophical meditation
venugeet: Kindles the gopis' longing love for the flute-playing Krishna
Transports the devotee to the divine Vrindavan leelas
Purifies the heart through meditation on Krishna's flute
Invokes spiritual bliss and transcendental joy
Traditionally recited for removal of worldly attachments and sorrows
Invokes the blessing of the vision of krishna's divine pastimes
virupaksha-stotram: Invokes the merit of pilgrimage to hampi virupaksha temple
Chanted to cleanse lifetimes of error before the lord of Hampi
Invokes the blessing of divine vision and spiritual wisdom
Connects with the sacred vibrations of Kishkindha Kshetra
vishnu-ashtakam: Invokes the blessing of divine blessings of lord vishnu
Traditionally recited for removal of sins and purifies the soul
Traditionally associated with peace, prosperity, and happiness
Helps in spiritual evolution and Self-realization
Eight verses recited with faith to draw the fulfilment Vishnu grants
vishnu-bhujanga-stotram: Composed in the elegant Bhujanga Prayata metre for melodious recitation
Invokes the blessing of divine grace of lord vishnu
Frees the devotee from the cycle of birth and death
Sung to dissolve sins and carry the soul toward liberation
Traditionally associated with peace and spiritual elevation
vishnu-chalisa: Invokes the blessing of the divine grace of lord vishnu
Traditionally associated with prosperity, wealth, and happiness
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sorrows and difficulties from life
Protects devotees from all dangers and enemies
Sought for the blessing of children and harmony in the household
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires when recited daily with devotion
vishnu-kavacham: Provides divine armor-like protection from all directions
Shields against enemies and negative forces
Chanted at dawn to strip away accumulated wrongdoing
Invokes freedom from diseases and ailments
Invokes the blessing of fearlessness and courage
Leads the devotee to Vishnuloka after death
vishnu-sahasranama-dhyanam: Meditation verses opening the thousand-name hymn of Vishnu
Fixes the mind on Vishnu's form before the Sahasranama recitation
Settles the heart in peace and auspicious focus before worship
Recited to purify the seeker and earn merit ahead of the names
Suited to Thursday worship and archana of Lord Vishnu
vishnu-sahasranama: Carries the soul beyond the cycle of birth and death to release
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins and bestows divine grace
Traditionally associated with wealth, prosperity, and success in all endeavors
Traditionally invoked for protection from all dangers and evil forces
Associated in tradition with diseases and bestows good health
Recited with devotion to fulfil the heart's lasting wishes
Traditionally associated with peace of mind and spiritual growth
Invokes the blessing of the blessings of lord vishnu and goddess lakshmi
vishnu-shodasha-nama: Provides specific divine protection for every life situation
Sixteen names cover all aspects of daily life
Extremely easy to memorize and recite daily
Chanted to clear the burden of sins gathered across many lives
Invokes vishnu's protection during travel, sleep, battles, and crises
Guides the soul to Vishnuloka, the abode of the Lord
vishnu-stotram: Comprehensive prayer invoking all aspects of Lord Vishnu's grace
Recited to wash away sins and cleanse the inner self
Traditionally associated with divine protection and blessings
Invokes peace of mind and freedom from fear
Invokes the blessing of wealth, health, and prosperity
Leads to spiritual liberation and moksha
Suitable for daily recitation by all devotees
vitthal-sahasranama: Contains thousand names of Lord Vitthal of Pandharpur
Most sacred stotra for Varkari tradition devotees
Invokes the brick-standing form of Vishnu at Pandharpur
Chanted on Ashadhi and Kartiki Ekadashi at the Vitthal shrine
vitthal-stotram: Invokes the merit of pandharpur pilgrimage
Invokes the blessing of krishna-prema and deep devotion (bhakti)
Connects with the Varkari devotional tradition
Recited so Vitthal's grace fulfils the devotee's heartfelt wishes
Especially powerful on Ashadhi and Kartiki Ekadashi
Purifies the heart through the bhakti rasa of abhang tradition
